headline: GOLD SLIDES TO $4,613, DOWN 20% FROM Q1 RECORD NEAR $5,600 — analysts call it an entry point
snippet: Gold fell to $4,613 per ounce on May 3, extending a correction that has now erased more than 20% from the first-quarter high near $5,600. The dollar index is at a two-month low, but institutional profit-taking continues to weigh on the metal. Analysts at Haywood describe the pullback as an attractive entry point, with structural demand — led by central bank buying and Asian retail investors — still firmly intact.

headline: SILVER HOLDS ABOVE $75 AS DOLLAR SINKS TO 2-MONTH LOW — JPMorgan targets $81 average for 2026
snippet: Silver climbed above $75 an ounce on May 1, outperforming gold as the U.S. dollar index fell to a two-month low following a surprise Iranian peace proposal and a sharp drop in oil prices. Silver is up 134% year-over-year and is on track for its sixth consecutive annual supply deficit. J.P. Morgan Research forecasts an average price of $81 per ounce across 2026.

headline: AGNICO EAGLE POSTS RECORD $1.71B QUARTERLY NET INCOME — $4,861/oz realized price crushes all-in costs
snippet: Agnico Eagle Mines reported record Q1 2026 adjusted net income of $1.71 billion on April 30, driven by a realized gold price of $4,861 per ounce against an all-in sustaining cost of just $1,483 per ounce. Free cash flow hit $732 million and net cash reached $2.9 billion. Fitch upgraded the company to A–. Full-year guidance of 3.3–3.5 million ounces remains intact.

headline: COT REPORT MAY 1: What speculative positioning in gold, silver, and the dollar signals next
snippet: GoldSeek's May 1 Commitments of Traders breakdown examines what large speculators and commercial hedgers are doing in gold and silver futures heading into May. COT structure analysis historically flags turning points in precious metals — particularly when commercials and large specs diverge from short-term price action during corrections like the current pullback.

headline: WORLD GOLD COUNCIL: Q1 demand value hits RECORD $193 BILLION — central banks net-buy 244 tonnes
snippet: Global gold demand reached 1,231 tonnes in Q1 2026, up 2% year-on-year in volume, but the surge in gold prices pushed total demand value to a record $193 billion — up 74% year-on-year. Central banks added a net 244 tonnes, their 15th consecutive quarter of net buying. Bar and coin demand surged 42% to 474 tonnes, the second-highest quarter on record. Published April 29, 2026.

headline: CHINA BANS SULPHURIC ACID EXPORTS FROM MAY — one-fifth of Chile's copper output at immediate risk
snippet: China has moved to halt sulphuric acid exports from May 2026, cutting off a critical input for roughly half of Chile's copper refining operations. Morgan Stanley estimates 1.1 million tonnes of Chilean leached copper production — about one-fifth of global mine supply — is now directly at risk. The ban stems from Middle East conflict disrupting sulphur shipments to China. Spot acid prices in Chile have already doubled since February.

headline: CHINA HIKES RARE EARTH PRICES 45% FOR Q2 — acid export ban deepens Western supply squeeze
snippet: China raised rare earth prices by 45% for Q2 2026 as domestic inventories fell to historically low levels. Beijing's simultaneous restriction on sulphuric acid exports — a key input in rare earth processing — further pressures supply chains outside China. Analysts point to MP Materials, USA Rare Earth, and Energy Fuels as the primary Western beneficiaries of tightening Chinese supply control.

headline: CHINA IMPORTS 836 TONNES OF SILVER IN MARCH — all-time record driven by solar and retail investors
snippet: China imported a record 836 tonnes of silver in March 2026, shattering all previous monthly highs, as the country's solar manufacturing boom and surging retail investment demand drove purchases far above seasonal averages. Early 2026 monthly totals have consistently exceeded 790 tonnes. The buying reinforces the structural supply-deficit thesis underpinning silver's move above $75 per ounce.

headline: GOLD MINER VALUATION DISCOUNT TO METAL HAS NEARLY CLOSED — sector may need to consolidate before next leg
snippet: After the 2025–2026 bull run, GDX and GDXJ have largely erased their historical discount to underlying metals, with leading producers' operating margins at cycle highs. Seeking Alpha analysts caution that miners may need a period of technical consolidation before a sustained next advance, noting rising capital expenditure and cost inflation beginning to narrow AISC margins for mid-tier and junior producers.

headline: MINING M&A HITS $21.6 BILLION IN Q1 — strongest deal quarter since 2023 as gold consolidation accelerates
snippet: Global mining M&A reached $21.6 billion across 121 transactions in Q1 2026, the strongest opening quarter since 2023. The full-year 2025 tally was a 13-year record at $93.7 billion. Gold is leading the current wave, with Agnico Eagle's $3.7 billion Finland consolidation and USA Rare Earth's $2.8 billion Serra Verde deal headlining the period. Critical minerals — particularly rare earths and copper — are drawing strategic acquirers seeking supply chain security.

headline: AI DATA CENTERS TO TURBOCHARGE URANIUM DEMAND — spot price climbs back above $100/lb as utilities return
snippet: Surging electricity demand from artificial intelligence data centres is adding a new structural demand driver to uranium markets already tightening from years of supply underinvestment. Spot uranium has moved back above $100 per pound as utilities re-enter the market following prolonged under-contracting. Asset managers cite Athabasca Basin juniors and Kazakh producers as the primary leverage plays.
